# feat(coc): sync loom 2.8.19 — 7 cross-SDK rule extensions (2.9.8)

**Commit**: `8600e8d37f37` — feat(coc): sync loom 2.8.19 — 7 cross-SDK rule extensions (2.9.8)

**Body**:

Single-cycle cross-SDK catch-up. Same 7 rule extensions the py template
received in 3.5.7, applied to rs template per loom global scope. Patterns
are language-agnostic (post-release audit discipline, keyspace-bump
invalidation sweep, multi-site kwarg plumbing, __all__ hygiene, structural
API-divergence disposition, phantom transitive deps, drive-the-data).

# 7 rule extensions

- rules/tenant-isolation.md +Rule 3a keyspace version bump invalidation
- rules/security.md MERGE +Multi-Site Kwarg Plumbing (rs Rust clauses
  preserved: Credential Comparison, Fail-Closed Defaults, Network
  Transport Hardening)
- rules/orphan-detection.md +Rule 6 __all__ hygiene (Python-specific
  pattern; rs USE template targets Python/Ruby binding consumers per
  manifest convention so Python examples land correctly)
- rules/cross-sdk-inspection.md +§3a structural API divergence
- rules/dependencies.md +Phantom Transitive Deps (multi-ecosystem — Rust
  Cargo.lock variant already covered)
- rules/agents.md MERGE +post-release audit row + Verify Deliverables
  (rs narrower specialist list preserved; no mcp-platform-specialist)
- rules/framework-first.md MERGE +Drive The Data Not The Dispatch
  (rs binding-focused anti-pattern table preserved)

Cargo pins unchanged (kailash-enterprise >= 3.18.0). No SDK release.
Hooks 12/12 on disk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
